steps.title: Read the contrast
steps.content: The opening questions invite readers to assume plants could not grow on Mars. The word but then reverses that expectation: researchers found that many seeds can germinate in simulated Martian soil when water is supplied, and some did better there than in Earth soil.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content: Choice A focuses on how soil conditions are simulated, Choice C makes water the unexpected issue, and Choice D shifts to soil composition. The passage is mainly presenting the surprising plant-growth finding itself. The answer is B.
